en.extract:
Lesson's motmot (Momotus lessonii) or the blue-diademed motmot, is a colorful near-passerine bird found in forests and woodlands of southern Mexico to western Panama.  This species and the blue-capped motmot, whooping motmot, Trinidad motmot, Amazonian motmot, and Andean motmot were all considered conspecific.


Description
The central crown is black and surrounded by a blue band. There is a black eyemask. The call is a low owl-like ooo-doot.
These birds often sit still, and in their dense forest habitat can be difficult to see, despite their size. They eat small prey such as insects and lizards, and will also regularly take fruit.
Like most of the Coraciiformes, motmots nest in tunnels in banks, laying about three or four white eggs.


Subspecies
The Lesson's motmot has three subspecies:

M. l. goldmani Nelson, 1900 -  southwestern Mexico to northern Guatemala
M. l. exiguus Ridgway, 1912 -  Campeche and Yucatán (southern Mexico)
M. l. lessonii Lesson, R., 1842 - Chiapas (southern Mexico) to western Panama


References

showing tail feathers]] '''Lesson's motmot''' ('''''Momotus lessonii''''') or the '''blue-diademed motmot''', is a colorful [[near-passerine]] [[bird]] found in forests and woodlands of southern [[Mexico]] to western [[Panama]]. This species and the [[blue-capped motmot]], [[whooping motmot]], [[Trinidad motmot]], [[Amazonian motmot]], and [[Andean motmot]] were all considered conspecific. ==Description== The central crown is black and surrounded by a blue band. There is a black eyemask. The call is a low [[owl|owl-like]] ''ooo-doot''. These birds often sit still, and in their dense forest habitat can be difficult to see, despite their size. They eat small prey such as insects and lizards, and will also regularly take fruit. Like most of the [[Coraciiformes]], motmots nest in tunnels in banks, laying about three or four white eggs. File:062 Lesson's motmot in San José Province Photo by Giles Laurent.jpg|Lesson's motmot in Costa Rica ==Subspecies== The Lesson's motmot has three subspecies: * ''M. l. goldmani'' [[Edward William Nelson|Nelson]], 1900 - southwestern [[Mexico]] to northern [[Guatemala]] * ''M. l. exiguus'' [[Robert Ridgway|Ridgway]], 1912 - [[Campeche]] and [[Yucatán]] (southern Mexico) * ''M. l. lessonii'' [[René Lesson|Lesson, R.]], 1842 - [[Chiapas]] (southern Mexico) to western [[Panama]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Taxonbar|from=Q3325204}} [[Category:Momotus|Lesson's motmot]] [[Category:Birds of Mexico]] [[Category:Birds of Central America]] [[Category:Birds of Belize]] [[Category:Birds of Guatemala]] [[Category:Birds of El Salvador]] [[Category:Birds of Honduras]] [[Category:Birds of Nicaragua]] [[Category:Birds of Costa Rica]] [[Category:Birds of Panama]] [[Category:Birds described in 1842|Lesson's motmot]] [[Category:Taxa named by René Lesson]]
